Mrs. Isabel Barquist 
San Jose Republican Women Federated 
355 Patton Avenue 
San Jose, CA 
95128 
Dear Mrs. Barquist: 
October 5, 1981 
This is in response to your letter dated September 14, 1981, in which 
you asked about the reporting requirements of the San Jose Republican 
Women Federated. 
Your specific question was whether the enclosed let-
ter (dated February 3, 1978) from Regina Kutchins is still applicable. 
The advice of Ms. Kutchins in still applicable, however there are a few 
points that I would like to clarify. 
The advice as set forth in Ms. 
Kutchins letter pertains to filing requirements pursuant to the 
California Political Reform Act. 
If the County Central Committee makes 
any contributions to Federal candidates (i.e., U.S. Senate or 
Congressional candidates) you should check with the Federal Elections 
Commission to clarify any possibly reporting requirements pursuant to 
Federal law. 
In regards to your contributions to the County Central Committee, if 
the central committee is allowing candidates to use supplies or office 
space, then the central committee is making contributions to candidates 
and your club's contributions to the central committee, even if those 
funds are used for overhead and ongoing operational expenses only, may 
be considered funds RearmarkedR for contributions. 
Therefore, in determining whether or not the San Jose Republican Women 
Federated may incur a filing requirement, you should determine exactly 
how the central committee uses funds received from individual clubs. 
However, you may use a reasonable method of allocation to determine 
when $500 of the club's funds has been used by the central committee as 
contributions. 
If I can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to con-
tact me. 
Yours Very Truly, 
~77~ 
Lynn Montgomery 
Political Reform Consultant 


F P P C~ 
;jfP 15 B 14 AH '81 
State of California 
355 Patton Ave., 
San Jose, CA. 
95128 
Sept. 14, 1981 
Fair Political Paactices Com., 
Sacramento, CA. 95804 
I enclose a Xerox copy of a letter received. 
from you several years ago. 
Recently my 
interpretation of this letter was questioned 
by some of my associates. 
To clarify: 
First, San Jose Republican Women collect B£ 
:tunds 
earmarked. for any political candidate. 
aecond~ to the best of our knowledge, none 
o:t the funds we contribute to the suppart of 
the Santa Clara County Central Committee are 
earmarked for use in making contributions to 
poli tical candidates. 
As far as we are aware 
all our contributions are used. for overhead 
expenses .. 
Our question is: Are there limits as to the 
amount in dollars and/or serv~ces that can be 
contributed towards the support of the Central 
Committee headquarters? 
Thanking you in advance for your attention 
to this matter, 
Yours truly 
'l~~~): 
Mrs. Isabel Barquist

sections.question: Are there limits as to the amount in dollars and/or services that can be contributed towards the support of the Central Committee headquarters? Is the advice from a previous letter dated February 3, 1978 from Regina Kutchins still applicable regarding reporting requirements of the San Jose Republican Women Federated?
sections.conclusion: The advice of Ms. Kutchins is still applicable. However, if the County Central Committee makes contributions to Federal candidates, the Federal Elections Commission should be consulted. If the central committee uses contributions for candidate supplies or office space, the club's contributions may be considered earmarked for contributions, requiring determination of how funds are used. A reasonable allocation method may be used to determine when $500 of club funds has been used by the central committee as contributions.
sections.question_synthetic: The San Jose Republican Women Federated seeks clarification on whether prior FPPC advice regarding their filing requirements under the California Political Reform Act remains applicable, and whether there are any dollar or service contribution limits for supporting the Santa Clara County Central Committee headquarters.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The prior advice remains applicable, but clubs must verify how the central committee uses their contributions and may need to check with the FEC if federal candidates receive support. Contributions used for candidate benefits may trigger filing requirements based on a $500 threshold using reasonable allocation methods.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated Sept 14, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (dated October 5, 1981) from Lynn Montgomery. The question section is synthesized from the client's stated inquiry. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION headers present; content extracted from letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are there limits as to the amount in dollars and/or services that can be contributed towards the support of the Central Committee headquarters? Is the advice from a previous letter dated February 3, 1978 from Regina Kutchins still applicable regarding reporting requirements of the San Jose Republican Women Federated?

CONCLUSION: The advice of Ms. Kutchins is still applicable. However, if the County Central Committee makes contributions to Federal candidates, the Federal Elections Commission should be consulted. If the central committee uses contributions for candidate supplies or office space, the club's contributions may be considered earmarked for contributions, requiring determination of how funds are used. A reasonable allocation method may be used to determine when $500 of club funds has been used by the central committee as contributions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the San Jose Republican Women Federated's inquiry about reporting requirements and contribution limits for their support of the Santa Clara County Central Committee, clarifying that prior advice still applies while adding guidance on earmarked funds and federal candidate contributions.
